---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Victor Ene"
date: "2025-05-04"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
install.packages("lmtest")
install.packages("readxl")
install.packages("mFilter")
install.packages("vars")
install.packages("dlm")
```

```{r}
library(forecast)
library(tseries)
library(zoo)
library(tsibble)
library(feasts)
library(lubridate)
library(e1071)
library(randomForest)
library(caret)
library(keras)
library(tensorflow)
library(ggplot2)
library(Metrics)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(lmtest)
library(readxl)
library(readr)
library(mFilter)
library(dlm)
library(vars)
```

#Preprocessing

```{r}
# Read the data, skipping the first 5 rows
fuel0 <- read_excel(
  path      = "fuel_price.xlsx",
  sheet     = "Table 1 Weekly",
  skip      = 5,
  col_names = TRUE
)

# Convert types ensure variables are in the correct type
fuel <- fuel0 |>
  rename(
    week_ending                = "Week ending",
    avg_fuel_price             = "Average fuel price",
    quantity_per_transaction   = "Estimated  quantity demand per transaction",
    avg_rd_sales               = "Average road fuel sales"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    week_ending                = as.Date(week_ending),
    avg_fuel_price             = as.numeric(avg_fuel_price),
    quantity_per_transaction   = as.numeric(quantity_per_transaction),
    avg_rd_sales               = as.numeric(avg_rd_sales)
  )

str(fuel)
```

```{r}
# Check for missing values
fuel |> summarise_all(~ sum(is.na(.)))

# Impute missing values via linear interpolation 
fuel <- fuel |>
  arrange(week_ending) |>
  mutate(
    # Linear interpolation for interior NAs
    avg_fuel_price            = na.approx(avg_fuel_price,            na.rm = FALSE),
    quantity_per_transaction  = na.approx(quantity_per_transaction,  na.rm = FALSE),
    avg_rd_sales              = na.approx(avg_rd_sales,              na.rm = FALSE)
  )

# Impute missing values via forward fill
fuel <- fuel |>
  arrange(week_ending) |>
  mutate(
    avg_rd_sales             = na.locf(avg_rd_sales,             na.rm = FALSE)
  )

fuel |> summarise_all(~ sum(is.na(.)))
```

#Outlier Detection

```{r}

# Visualize outliers using a boxplot
ggplot(fuel, aes(x = week_ending, y = avg_fuel_price)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.colour = "red", outlier.size = 3) + 
  labs(title = "Boxplot of Fuel Price highlighting outliers", x = "Week Ending", y = "Fuel Price") +
  theme_minimal()

# Calculate IQR values
iqr_vals <- IQR(fuel$avg_fuel_price, na.rm = TRUE)
q1       <- quantile(fuel$avg_fuel_price, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
q3       <- quantile(fuel$avg_fuel_price, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
lower_f  <- q1 - 1.5 * iqr_vals
upper_f  <- q3 + 1.5 * iqr_vals

# Identify outlier indices
out_idx <- which(fuel$avg_fuel_price < lower_f | fuel$avg_fuel_price > upper_f)

# Visualise IQR-based outliers on the interpolated series
ggplot(fuel, aes(week_ending, avg_fuel_price)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(data = fuel[out_idx, ], aes(week_ending, avg_fuel_price), colour = "red") +
  labs(title = "Fuel Price with IQR-Based Outliers Highlighted")

# replace the outliers with NA and apply linear interpolation
fuel$avg_fuel_price[out_idx] <- NA  
fuel$avg_fuel_price <- zoo::na.approx(fuel$avg_fuel_price, na.rm = FALSE)


```

##Duplicate values

```{r}
# Find duplicates
duplicates <- fuel |>
  group_by(week_ending) |>
  filter(n() > 1)

print(duplicates)

fuel <- fuel |>
  group_by(week_ending) |>
  summarise(
    avg_fuel_price           = mean(avg_fuel_price, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_rd_sales             = mean(avg_rd_sales, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  ungroup()

# Check again for duplicates after aggregation 
duplicates_after <- fuel |>
  group_by(week_ending) |>
  filter(n() > 1)

print(duplicates_after)

```

```{r}
# Convert cleaned data to ts
fuel_ts <- ts(fuel$avg_fuel_price, frequency = 52, start = c(2021, 4))
 

#visualise the time series
autoplot(fuel_ts) +
  labs(
    title = "Cleaned Weekly Average Fuel Price",
    x     = "Week Ending",
    y     = "Price (pence per litre)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

## Decomposition

```{r}
# STL decomposition
d_compose <- stl(fuel_ts, s.window = "periodic")
autoplot(d_compose) +
  labs(
    title = "STL Decomposition",
    x     = "Weeks"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Hodrick–Prescott filter decomposition
hp <- hpfilter(fuel_ts, type = "lambda", freq = 129600)
autoplot(fuel_ts, series = "Cleaned") +
  autolayer(hp$trend, series = "HP Trend") +
  autolayer(hp$cycle, series = "HP Cycle") +
  labs(
    title = "HP Filter Decomposition",
    x     = "Week"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

## Stationarity

```{r}
adf.test(fuel_ts)

fuel_ts_diff <- diff(fuel_ts)
adf.test(fuel_ts_diff)

```

## acf / pacf plots

```{r}
#par(mfrow = c(1,2))
acf(fuel_ts_diff)
pacf(fuel_ts_diff)
```

## Train/Test Split

```{r}
# Total length of the fuel time series
n <- length(fuel_ts_diff)

# Split target variable (avg_fuel_price)
train <- window(fuel_ts_diff, end = time(fuel_ts_diff)[floor(0.8 * n)])
test <- window(fuel_ts_diff, start = time(fuel_ts_diff)[floor(0.8 * n) + 1])

```

## Models

```{r}
# Apply SARIMA using auto.arima() function
sarima_model <- auto.arima(train, seasonal = TRUE)

# Forecast using the SARIMA model
sarima_forecast <- forecast(sarima_model, h = length(test))

# Plot the forecast
plot(sarima_forecast)
```

```{r}
# Create comparison data frame
sarima_df <- data.frame(
  Week = 1:length(test),
  Actual = as.numeric(test),
  Predicted = as.numeric(sarima_forecast$mean)
)

ggplot(sarima_df, aes(x = Week)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Actual, colour = "Actual")) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Predicted, colour = "Predicted")) +
  labs(
    title = "SARIMA: Actual vs Predicted Fuel Prices",
    x = "Test Week",
    y = "Differenced Fuel Price"
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Actual" = "black", "Predicted" = "red")) +
  theme_minimal()

```

### Holt-Winters Model

```{r}
hw_model <- HoltWinters(train)
hw_forecast <- forecast(hw_model, h = length(test))
plot(hw_forecast)
```

### STLF

```{r}
stlf_model <- stlf(train, h = length(test))
stlf_forecast <- stlf_model
plot(stlf_forecast)

```

##Multivariate analysis

```{r}
# Load the external data (oil prices)
external_data <- read_csv("DCOILBRENTEU.csv")

external_data <- external_data |>
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date, format = "%Y-%m-%d"))

sum(is.na(external_data$DCOILBRENTEU)) 

# Handle missing values
external_data <- external_data |>
  mutate(DCOILBRENTEU = na.approx(DCOILBRENTEU, na.rm = FALSE))

sum(is.na(external_data$DCOILBRENTEU))


# Standardise week alignment: set both datasets to the same weekday 
fuel <- fuel |>
  mutate(week_ending = floor_date(week_ending, unit = "week", week_start = 5))

external_weekly <- external_data |>
  mutate(week_ending = floor_date(observation_date, unit = "week", week_start = 5)) |>
  group_by(week_ending) |>
  summarise(Oil_Price = mean(DCOILBRENTEU, na.rm = TRUE))

# Merge
merged_data <- left_join(fuel, external_weekly, by = "week_ending")

head(merged_data)
```

```{r}
# Convert merged data to a time series object
merged_ts <- ts(merged_data[, c("avg_fuel_price", "avg_rd_sales", "Oil_Price")], frequency = 52, start = c(2021, 4))  # Weekly data, starting from week 4 of 2021

# Check the structure of the new time series object
str(merged_ts)
sum(is.na(merged_ts))

# Apply ADF test for stationarity check
adf.test(merged_ts[, "avg_fuel_price"]) 
adf.test(merged_ts[, "avg_rd_sales"])
adf.test(merged_ts[, "Oil_Price"])

# Convert the time series object to a data frame
merged_ts_df <- data.frame(merged_ts)

# Apply differencing to avg_fuel_price and Oil_Price (but not avg_rd_sales)
# Add NA for the first value to maintain consistency with the original length
merged_ts_diff <- merged_ts_df %>%
  mutate(
    avg_fuel_price = c(NA, diff(avg_fuel_price, differences = 1)),  # Add NA for the first value
    Oil_Price  = c(NA, diff(Oil_Price, differences = 1))         # Add NA for the first value
  ) %>%
  drop_na()  # Remove NA values generated by differencing

# Convert the resulting data back into a time series object
merged_ts_diff <- ts(merged_ts_diff[, c("avg_fuel_price", "avg_rd_sales", "Oil_Price")], frequency = 52, start = c(2021, 4))

# Check the structure of the new time series object
str(merged_ts_diff)
sum(is.na(merged_ts_diff))

```

## Granger casuality

```{r}
# Create differenced data for Granger causality test
granger_df <- as.data.frame(merged_ts_diff) %>%
  dplyr::select(avg_fuel_price, Oil_Price) %>%
  drop_na()  

# Granger causality test
granger_test_oil_to_fuel <- grangertest(granger_df$avg_fuel_price ~ granger_df$Oil_Price, order = 2)
print(granger_test_oil_to_fuel)

```

```{r}
n <- nrow(merged_ts_diff)
split <- floor(0.8 * n)

train_ts <- merged_ts_diff[1:split, ]
test_ts  <- merged_ts_diff[(split + 1):n, ]
```

```{r}
# SARIMAX 
sarimax_model <- auto.arima(train_ts[, "avg_fuel_price"], xreg = train_ts[, "Oil_Price"])
sarimax_forecast <- forecast(sarimax_model, xreg = test_ts[, "Oil_Price"])
sarimax_pred <- sarimax_forecast$mean

```

```{r}
# Fit SARIMAX model with both oil prices and sales volume as exogenous variables
sarimax_with_sales <- auto.arima(train_ts[, "avg_fuel_price"], xreg = train_ts[, c("avg_rd_sales", "Oil_Price")], seasonal = TRUE)
sarimax_with_sales_forecast <- forecast(sarimax_with_sales, xreg = test_ts[, c("avg_rd_sales", "Oil_Price")])

# Fit SARIMAX model with only oil prices as the exogenous variable
sarimax_no_sales <- auto.arima(train_ts[, "avg_fuel_price"], xreg = train_ts[, "Oil_Price", drop = FALSE], seasonal = TRUE)
sarimax_no_sales_forecast <- forecast(sarimax_no_sales, xreg = test_ts[, "Oil_Price", drop = FALSE])

actual_f <- test_ts[, "avg_fuel_price"]
rmse_sarimax_with_sales <- rmse(actual_f, sarimax_with_sales_forecast$mean[1:length(actual_f)])
rmse_sarimax_no_sales <- rmse(actual_f, sarimax_no_sales_forecast$mean[1:length(actual_f)])

compare_sales_df <- data.frame(
  Model = c("With Sales Volume", "Without Sales Volume"),
  RMSE = c(rmse_sarimax_with_sales, rmse_sarimax_no_sales)
)
print(compare_sales_df)

```

```{r}
build_dlm <- function(par) {
  dlmModPoly(order = 1, dV = exp(par[1]), dW = exp(par[2]))
}

dlm_fit <- dlmMLE(train_ts[, "avg_fuel_price"], parm = c(1, 1), build = build_dlm)
model_dlm <- build_dlm(dlm_fit$par)
dlm_forecast <- dlmForecast(model_dlm, nAhead = nrow(test_ts))
dlm_pred <- sapply(dlm_forecast$a, function(x) x[1])

```

```{r}
VARselect(train_ts, lag.max = 10, type = "const")  # choose best lag
model_var <- VAR(train_ts, p = 3)  
var_forecast <- predict(model_var, n.ahead = nrow(test_ts))
var_pred <- var_forecast$fcst$avg_fuel_price[, "fcst"]  

```

```{r}
# Convert merged_ts_diff to a data frame
merged_ts_diff_df <- data.frame(merged_ts_diff)

# Apply lag and create new variables (lag1, lag2, oil_lag1)
svr_df <- merged_ts_diff_df %>%
  mutate(
    lag1 = lag(avg_fuel_price, 1),      # Lag of avg_fuel_price by 1
    lag2 = lag(avg_fuel_price, 2),      # Lag of avg_fuel_price by 2
    oil_lag1 = lag(Oil_Price, 1)        # Lag of Oil_Price by 1
  ) %>%
  drop_na()  # Drop NA values generated by lagging

head(svr_df)

train_svr <- svr_df[1:floor(0.8 * nrow(svr_df)), ]
test_svr <- svr_df[(floor(0.8 * nrow(svr_df)) + 1):nrow(svr_df), ]

# Train the SVR model with lagged fuel price and oil price as exogenous variables
svr_model <- svm(avg_fuel_price ~ lag1 + lag2 + oil_lag1, data = train_svr)
svr_pred <- predict(svr_model, newdata = test_svr)

```

```{r}
# Random Forest
rf_model <- randomForest(avg_fuel_price ~ lag1 + lag2 + oil_lag1, data = train_svr)
rf_pred <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_svr)


```

##model evaluation

```{r}

actual_univariate   <- as.numeric(test)
actual_multivariate <- test_ts[, "avg_fuel_price"]
actual_svr_rf       <- test_svr$avg_fuel_price

# Slice predictions
sarima_pred    <- sarima_forecast$mean[1:length(actual_univariate)]
hw_pred        <- hw_forecast$mean[1:length(actual_univariate)]
stlf_pred      <- stlf_forecast$mean[1:length(actual_univariate)]
var_pred       <- var_pred[1:length(actual_multivariate)]
dlm_pred       <- dlm_pred[1:length(actual_multivariate)]
sarimax_pred   <- sarimax_pred[1:length(actual_multivariate)]
svr_pred       <- svr_pred[1:length(actual_svr_rf)]
rf_pred        <- rf_pred[1:length(actual_svr_rf)]

# RMSE
rmse_values <- c(
  rmse(actual_univariate, sarima_pred),
  rmse(actual_univariate, hw_pred),
  rmse(actual_univariate, stlf_pred),
  rmse(actual_multivariate, var_pred),
  rmse(actual_multivariate, dlm_pred),
  rmse(actual_multivariate, sarimax_pred),
  rmse(actual_svr_rf, svr_pred),
  rmse(actual_svr_rf, rf_pred)
)
# MAE
mae_values <- c(
  mae(actual_univariate, sarima_pred),
  mae(actual_univariate, hw_pred),
  mae(actual_univariate, stlf_pred),
  mae(actual_multivariate, var_pred),
  mae(actual_multivariate, dlm_pred),
  mae(actual_multivariate, sarimax_pred),
  mae(actual_svr_rf, svr_pred),
  mae(actual_svr_rf, rf_pred)
)

model_names <- c("SARIMA", "Holt-Winters", "STLF",
                 "VAR", "DLM", "SARIMAX", "SVR", "Random Forest")

accuracy_df <- data.frame(
  Model = model_names,
  RMSE  = round(rmse_values, 4),
  MAE   = round(mae_values, 4)
)

accuracy_df
# --- Step 2: Visualise Accuracy (Bar Plot by RMSE) ---
ggplot(accuracy_df, aes(x = reorder(Model, RMSE), y = RMSE)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "lightblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Model Comparison by RMSE",
    x = "Model",
    y = "Root Mean Squared Error"
  ) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(RMSE, 2)), hjust = -0.2, size = 3.5) +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
# Step 3: Actual vs Predicted for SVR
svr_df_plot <- data.frame(
  Week = 1:length(actual_svr_rf),
  Actual = actual_svr_rf,
  Predicted = svr_pred
)

ggplot(svr_df_plot, aes(x = Week)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Actual, colour = "Actual")) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Predicted, colour = "Predicted")) +
  labs(
    title = "SVR: Actual vs Predicted Fuel Prices",
    x = "Test Week",
    y = "Differenced Fuel Price"
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Actual" = "black", "Predicted" = "red")) +
  theme_minimal()

```

## forecasting using best mode;

```{r}
# Load the merged data 
# Prepare features: lagging `avg_fuel_price` and `Oil_Price`
svr_df <- merged_data %>%
  mutate(
    lag1 = lag(avg_fuel_price, 1),
    lag2 = lag(avg_fuel_price, 2),
    oil_lag1 = lag(Oil_Price, 1)
  ) %>%
  drop_na() 

# Train SVR model using the entire dataset
svr_model <- svm(avg_fuel_price ~ lag1 + lag2 + oil_lag1, data = svr_df)

# Forecast the next 24 weeks using recursive prediction
# Start with the last available input row (for recursive prediction)
last_row <- tail(svr_df, 1)

# Pre-allocate a vector for the 24-week forecast
svr_forecast_24 <- numeric(24)

# Perform the recursive forecast for 24 weeks ahead
for (i in 1:24) {
  new_input <- data.frame(
    lag1 = last_row$lag1,
    lag2 = last_row$lag2,
    oil_lag1 = last_row$oil_lag1
  )
  
  # Predict the next value
  pred_next <- predict(svr_model, new_input)
  svr_forecast_24[i] <- pred_next
  
  # Update lags for the next prediction
  last_row$lag2 <- last_row$lag1
  last_row$lag1 <- pred_next
  last_row$oil_lag1 <- last_row$oil_lag1 # Optionally update oil prices if forecasts available
}

# Time index for the next 24 weeks
start_week <- max(merged_data$week_ending, na.rm = TRUE) + 7  # Start from the next week
future_weeks <- seq.Date(from = start_week, by = "week", length.out = 24)

# Combine the forecasted values with the corresponding weeks
svr_forecast_df <- data.frame(
  Week_Ending = future_weeks,
  Forecasted_Fuel_Price = svr_forecast_24
)

# Print forecasted values
print(svr_forecast_df)

```

```{r}
# Combine historical and forecasted data for visualization
historical_data <- data.frame(
  Week_Ending = merged_data$week_ending,
  Fuel_Price = merged_data$avg_fuel_price
)

# Combine historical data and forecasted data
forecasted_data <- data.frame(
  Week_Ending = future_weeks,
  Fuel_Price = svr_forecast_24
)

# Combine historical and forecasted data into a single data frame
combined_data <- bind_rows(
  historical_data %>% mutate(Type = "Historical"),
  forecasted_data %>% mutate(Type = "Forecasted")
)

# Visualize both historical and forecasted data
ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = Week_Ending, y = Fuel_Price, colour = Type)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "SVR Forecast vs Historical Fuel Prices",
    x = "Week Ending",
    y = "Fuel Price",
    colour = "Data Type"
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("black", "darkgreen")) +
  theme_minimal()

```
